mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, prompting risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rates signal central bank tightening, creating headwinds for equity valuations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where both CPIAUCSL > 325 and FEDFUNDS > 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,300 thousand units signals weak demand for residential construction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances during late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above 102.0 signals robust manufacturing activity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through improved corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of triggered instances during early-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in real sales (RSAFS) below 730 billion dollars signals weak consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days during monetary tightening regimes.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) below 159,000 thousand signals weak labor market performance, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during high-inflation environments.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances during high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above 102.0 combined with a decline in manufacturing inventories (RSAFS) below 730 billion dollars signals overproduction and weak demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO > 102.0 and RSAFS < 730.
